headline: Mantel and Aalborg CSP sign strategic supply agreement for modular molten-salt tank systems
summary: High-temperature capture developer Mantel signed a strategic supply agreement with Danish thermal-engineering firm Aalborg CSP to develop a standardised, road-transportable modular plant design for the molten-salt tank subsystem of Mantel's molten-borate carbon capture platform, which it says cuts capture energy losses ~97% at under half the industry-average cost per tonne.
co2crc_note: Novel high-temperature liquid-phase (molten-borate) capture with modularised, standardised plant design bears on the capture-cost economics of Australian hard-to-abate deployment.
extractor_note: Date confirmed via Manila Times URL date-path and same-day GlobeNewswire syndication; article page not fetchable (egress 403).

headline: Tencent signs first carbon-removal credit deal outside China
summary: Tencent signed a 10-year agreement to buy 300,000 carbon-removal credits from Singapore-based developer Thryve.Earth, sourced from an agroforestry project on Sulawesi, Indonesia — its first CDR purchase outside China; Google took 260,000 and McKinsey 75,000 credits from the same project.
co2crc_note: A major Chinese hyperscaler entering the international voluntary CDR market is a demand-side signal for the broader removals market Australian CCS/CDR players sell into.
extractor_note: Nature-based agroforestry removal, not engineered/geological CCS; included as a CDR-market demand signal. Date confirmed via Bloomberg URL date-token and Google Alerts pipeline timestamp; page not fetchable (egress 403).

headline: Investment into GHG-capture technologies fell 49% year-on-year in H1 2026
summary: Carbon Pulse reported that capital deployed into greenhouse-gas capture technologies shrank 49% year-on-year across the first six months of 2026, a sharp pullback pointing to tightening financing conditions for the capture sector despite an expanding project pipeline.
co2crc_note: A halving of capture investment flows shapes the funding environment for Australian CCS project developers and technology providers.
extractor_note: Market-aggregate percentage, no discrete money figure (amount null, commitment_status na). Date per Carbon Pulse CP Daily 8 Jul; paywalled and not fetchable (egress 403).

headline: Wood Mackenzie: CCUS capacity keeps growing despite project delays and policy challenges
summary: Wood Mackenzie analysis found global CCUS capacity under construction rose 9% and capacity in advanced development rose 23% between Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, even as delays, weak incentives and local opposition persist; WoodMac tracks ~1,200 announced projects with only ~10% operational, and holds a base case rising from ~91 Mtpa today toward ~3 Gtpa by 2060.
co2crc_note: A capacity-growth counterpoint to the investment pullback — useful context for gauging the near-term trajectory of the global CCS build-out Australian projects sit within.
extractor_note: Analyst market assessment (amount null, commitment_status na). Reported by Pipeline & Gas Journal 8 Jul citing Wood Mackenzie; pages not fetchable (egress 403).

headline: Australian opinion: CCS is helping fossil-fuel companies dump risk onto the public
summary: An Australian opinion piece argues that carbon capture and storage functions primarily to transfer long-term liability and risk from fossil-fuel producers onto the public, citing the gap between promised and delivered capture rates at Australian projects — a social-licence critique aimed at the domestic gas sector.
co2crc_note: Direct Australian social-licence/reputational commentary on CCS — CO2CRC's home-country operating environment.
extractor_note: Date inferred from URL date-slug (260708); article page not fetchable (egress 403).

headline: Fraser Institute study questions reliance on CCUS as a core climate policy
summary: A July 2026 Fraser Institute study by Kenneth P. Green argues CCUS is likely to fall short as a climate-policy tool: it routinely captures below 80% of predicted levels, needs infrastructure as large as oil-and-gas production itself, and to date ~73% of CCUS use has served enhanced oil recovery rather than climate mitigation, leaving long-term large-scale sequestration economics unproven.
co2crc_note: A think-tank critique of CCUS climate efficacy that feeds the sceptic narrative CO2CRC must engage on social-licence and public-policy grounds.
extractor_note: Think-tank (free-market) study; org_type 'industry-body' used as closest for a policy think-tank. July 2026 Fraser Institute report, coverage 8 Jul; pages not fetchable (egress 403).
